content.text:
## Task: Drop the Actions header label and tag the browse table with a 'browse-table' class; update affected test assertions
## Step: review-tests

Make the Actions column have NO visible header label and give the browse table a distinguishing class so CSS (added in task-1) can scope column alignment to it without affecting the search table. Edit these files:

1. `src/app/render-browse.ts`:
   - Change the header array from `buildTable(['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', 'Actions'])` to `buildTable(['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', ''])` — keep FOUR `<th>` (the 4th empty) so the header row column count still matches the 4 `<td>` per body row and `:nth-child` column targeting stays uniform. The empty string produces an empty `<th>` (no visible label), satisfying 'the actions column doesn't need a header now'.
   - Immediately after building the table, add the class: `table.classList.add('browse-table');` (so `className` becomes `'results-table browse-table'`).
   - Update the JSDoc comment at the top of the file that says 'a four-column table (Name | Size | Modified | Actions)' to reflect that the Actions header is now blank / actions are in a per-row dropdown.

2. `src/app/render-browse.test.ts` (around line 58): change the header assertion from `expect(headers).toEqual(['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', 'Actions']);` to `expect(headers).toEqual(['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', '']);`.

3. `src/app/render-orchestration.test.ts` (lines ~177 and ~209): change both `expect(headers).toEqual(['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', 'Actions']);` to `['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', '']`.

4. `src/app.characterization.test.ts`:
   - Line ~232: change the header assertion to `['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', '']`.
   - Line ~222: the assertion `expect(results.querySelector('table')!.className).toBe('results-table');` now fails because the browse table also carries `browse-table`. Change it to assert both classes, e.g. `const t = results.querySelector('table')!; expect(t.classList.contains('results-table')).toBe(true); expect(t.classList.contains('browse-table')).toBe(true);`.

Do NOT touch `dom-builders.ts` (the dropdown itself is a separate task) and do NOT touch `render-search.ts` or its tests (the search table keeps header `['Name','Path','Size','Modified']` and NO `browse-table` class).

IMPORTANT: leave the existing 'Actions column' behavior tests in `render-browse.test.ts` and the `makeActionButton` tests in `app.characterization.test.ts` UNCHANGED — they must still pass after this task because `makeBrowseRow` still renders the inline Download/Delete/Move/Copy controls at this point. If any of them fail, you have over-edited; revert and only change the header-array and table-class concerns.

Verification: run `npx vitest run src/app/render-browse.test.ts src/app/render-orchestration.test.ts src/app.characterization.test.ts`. Every test must pass. Specifically confirm: the four header-array assertions now expect `['Name','Size','Modified','']`, the browse table carries both `results-table` and `browse-table`, the search table still has header `['Name','Path','Size','Modified']` and no `browse-table` class, and all existing Actions-column behavior tests (Download/Delete/Move/Copy, error surfacing) still pass unchanged.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The test assertions in all three test files have been correctly updated to match the intended production changes (empty Actions header, browse-table class). However, the production code in src/app/render-browse.ts has NOT been updated yet, causing 7 test failures. Specifically: (1) buildTable still receives ['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', 'Actions'] instead of ['Name', 'Size', 'Modified', ''], (2) table.classList.add('browse-table') is not called after building the table, and (3) the JSDoc comment still mentions 'Actions'. Once the production code changes are applied per the task description, all 88 tests should pass. The 81 passing tests confirm that existing Actions column behavior tests (Download/Delete/Move/Copy, error surfacing) are unaffected by these test updates.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }
